description: Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identity number issued by UIDAI to residents of India. It serves as proof of identity and address and is required for most government services, bank accounts, and subsidies.
requiredDocuments: Proof of Identity (Passport, PAN Card, Voter ID, or Driving License)
Proof of Address (Utility bill, Bank passbook, Ration card, or Passport)
Proof of Date of Birth (Birth certificate, SSLC certificate, or Passport)
Proof of Relationship (for Head of Family-based address proof): Ration Card, PDS Card
steps: Visit the nearest Aadhaar Enrollment/Update Centre or book an appointment at uidai.gov.in
Carry original documents for verification (photocopies not accepted alone)
Fill the enrollment/update form at the centre
Provide biometrics (fingerprints, iris scan) and photograph
Collect the acknowledgment slip with your Enrollment ID (EID)
Track status online at uidai.gov.in using the EID
Aadhaar will be delivered by post; you can also download e-Aadhaar online
whereToApply: Nearest Aadhaar Enrollment Centre (find on uidai.gov.in) or Common Service Centre (CSC)
estimatedTime: 15–30 days for new enrollment; 7–15 days for updates
fees: Free for new enrollment. Update: ₹50 per update request (biometric or demographic)

description: A Ration Card is an official document issued by state governments that entitles holders to purchase subsidized food grains and other essential commodities from Public Distribution System (PDS) shops.
requiredDocuments: Application form (available at Taluk/Tehsil office or state food department website)
Aadhaar Card of all family members
Proof of Address (Electricity bill, rental agreement, or property documents)
Income Certificate or salary slip
Passport-size photographs of the head of family
Bank account details
Gas connection details (if any)
steps: Obtain the application form from your local Taluk/Tehsil Supply Office or download from the state PDS portal
Fill the form with details of all family members
Attach self-attested copies of required documents
Submit at the Taluk/Tehsil Supply Office or through the state's online PDS portal
A field verification may be conducted by a government official
Collect the Ration Card from the same office once approved, or it will be posted
whereToApply: Taluk/Tehsil Supply Office or your state's online PDS/Food & Civil Supplies portal
fees: Typically ₹5 to ₹45 depending on the state (some states issue free)

description: A Birth Certificate is an official record of a person's birth, issued by the local municipal authority. It is a vital document needed for school admission, passport, Aadhaar, and various other purposes.
requiredDocuments: Hospital discharge slip / birth report from the hospital
Aadhaar Card of both parents
Marriage Certificate of parents (if available)
Proof of Address (any utility bill or Aadhaar)
Affidavit (if registering after 21 days of birth — late registration)
steps: Most hospitals now register births directly — check with your hospital
If not auto-registered, visit your local Municipal Corporation / Gram Panchayat office
Fill the birth registration form
Submit the required documents
For delayed registration (after 21 days), an affidavit and magistrate order may be needed
Collect the Birth Certificate from the issuing office or download from your state's CRS portal
whereToApply: Local Municipal Corporation, Nagar Palika, or Gram Panchayat office; many states offer online registration via crsorgi.gov.in
estimatedTime: 7–21 days (immediate if within 21 days of birth at hospital)
fees: Free if registered within 21 days; ₹10–₹50 for delayed registration (varies by state)

description: A Death Certificate is an official document stating the cause, date, and place of death. It is required for property transfer, insurance claims, pension settlement, and legal matters.
requiredDocuments: Death report from the hospital (if death occurred at hospital)
Aadhaar Card of the deceased (if available)
Proof of Identity of the informant/applicant
Proof of Address of the deceased
Cremation/Burial certificate (if applicable)
FIR copy (in case of unnatural death)
steps: Hospitals usually initiate death registration — check with the hospital
If not auto-registered, visit the local Municipal/Panchayat office within 21 days
Fill the death registration form
Submit required documents
For delayed registration, an affidavit and magistrate order may be needed
Collect the Death Certificate from the issuing office
whereToApply: Local Municipal Corporation, Nagar Palika, or Gram Panchayat office; online via crsorgi.gov.in in some states
fees: Free if registered within 21 days; ₹10–₹50 for delayed registration

description: An Income Certificate certifies the annual income of a person or family. It is needed for scholarships, fee concessions, reservation benefits, subsidized housing, and various government schemes.
requiredDocuments: Application form
Aadhaar Card
Ration Card
Salary slip / Income proof (employer letter, IT returns, or self-declaration)
Self-declaration affidavit (for self-employed / no formal income proof)
Passport-size photograph
steps: Obtain the application form from the Tehsildar/Taluk office or state e-services portal
Fill the form and attach required documents
Submit at the Tehsildar/Revenue office or online via your state's e-District portal
A revenue official may conduct a field inquiry
Collect the certificate from the same office or download online
whereToApply: Tehsildar / Taluk office / MeeSeva / CSC / state e-District portal
fees: ₹10–₹50 (varies by state; free in some states through e-District)

description: A Caste Certificate certifies that a person belongs to a particular Scheduled Caste (SC), Scheduled Tribe (ST), or Other Backward Class (OBC). It is required for reservation benefits in education and employment.
requiredDocuments: Application form
Aadhaar Card
Ration Card
Parent's Caste Certificate (if available)
School Transfer Certificate / Leaving Certificate mentioning caste
Proof of Address
Self-declaration affidavit (if no parental certificate)
Passport-size photograph
steps: Obtain the application form from Tehsildar office or state e-services portal
Fill the form and attach required documents
Submit at the Tehsildar/Revenue office or online through e-District portal
A field inquiry will be conducted by the revenue department
After verification, the certificate is issued
Collect from the Tehsildar office or download from the e-District portal
whereToApply: Tehsildar / Revenue / District office / state e-District portal / MeeSeva / CSC

description: A Domicile or Residence Certificate proves that a person is a resident of a particular state. It is required for state-level educational admissions, government jobs, and other state-specific benefits.
requiredDocuments: Application form
Aadhaar Card
Ration Card
Proof of residing in the state for the required duration (utility bills, rent agreement, property documents)
School/College certificates showing education in the state
Passport-size photograph
Self-declaration affidavit
steps: Obtain the form from the Tehsildar/Revenue office or state e-services portal
Fill the form with residential history details
Attach all supporting documents
Submit at the Tehsildar/Revenue office or online
A field verification may be done by the revenue department
Collect the certificate after approval
whereToApply: Tehsildar / Revenue / District office / state e-District portal

description: Property Tax is an annual tax levied by the local Municipal Corporation / Panchayat on residential and commercial properties. Timely payment avoids penalties and is required for property-related legal work.
requiredDocuments: Property Tax Identification Number (PTIN) or Property ID / Assessment Number
Previous tax payment receipts (if available)
Property ownership documents (for first-time registration)
steps: Find your Property Tax ID from previous receipts or by visiting the municipal office
Visit your Municipal Corporation's website or office
Enter your Property ID and view the outstanding tax amount
Pay online via net banking, UPI, or credit/debit card on the municipal portal
Alternatively, pay in person at the municipal office or authorized collection centres
Download and save the payment receipt
whereToApply: Local Municipal Corporation / Nagar Palika website or office; many cities support online payment through their portals
fees: Varies based on property size, location, type, and municipal rates. Early payment may get 5–10% discount in many cities.

description: Apply for a new water supply connection from your local water board / municipal authority for your residential or commercial property.
requiredDocuments: Application form
Proof of property ownership (sale deed, property tax receipt, or allotment letter)
Aadhaar Card / ID proof
Building plan approval (for new constructions)
No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the housing society (if in apartment)
Passport-size photograph
steps: Visit the local Water Board / Municipal Corporation office or their website
Fill the new water connection application form
Attach required documents and photographs
Pay the application fee and connection charges
A site inspection will be conducted by the water board
After approval, the connection will be installed
Collect the consumer number / connection ID
whereToApply: Local Water Board / Jal Board / Municipal Corporation office or their online portal
fees: ₹500–₹5,000+ depending on connection type (domestic/commercial), pipe diameter, and city

description: Pay your electricity bills or apply for a new electricity connection from your state's power distribution company (DISCOM).
requiredDocuments: For bill payment: Consumer number / account number
For new connection: Application form, Aadhaar Card, Proof of ownership/tenancy, Building plan approval, Passport-size photograph
Wiring completion certificate from a licensed electrician (for new connection)
steps: Bill Payment: Visit your DISCOM's website/app, enter consumer number, pay via UPI/card/net banking
New Connection: Visit the local DISCOM office or apply online
Fill the new connection application form
Submit required documents and pay the application and security deposit fees
A site inspection will be conducted
Meter will be installed after approval
Note your new consumer number for future payments
whereToApply: State DISCOM office or website (e.g., TSSPDCL, BESCOM, MSEDCL, BSES, UPPCL)
estimatedTime: Bill payment: Immediate. New connection: 7–30 days
fees: Bill payment: As per consumption. New connection: ₹500–₹5,000+ (varies by state, load requirement, and connection type)

description: The Voter ID Card (EPIC - Electoral Photo Identity Card) is issued by the Election Commission of India and is required to vote in elections. It also serves as a valid proof of identity and address.
requiredDocuments: Form 6 (for new registration) or Form 8 (for corrections/updates)
Proof of Age (Birth certificate, School leaving certificate, or Passport)
Proof of Address (Aadhaar, Utility bill, Bank passbook, or Ration card)
Passport-size photograph
Aadhaar Card (optional but recommended for linking)
steps: Visit the National Voters' Service Portal (voters.eci.gov.in) or download the Voter Helpline App
Register/login and fill Form 6 for new registration or Form 8 for updates
Upload scanned copies of required documents and photograph
Submit the application online or at the nearest Electoral Registration Office (ERO)
A Booth Level Officer (BLO) may visit for verification
After verification, your name is added to the electoral roll
Download e-EPIC online or collect the physical card from the ERO
whereToApply: National Voters' Service Portal (voters.eci.gov.in), Voter Helpline App, or nearest Electoral Registration Office

description: PAN (Permanent Account Number) is a 10-digit alphanumeric identifier issued by the Income Tax Department. It is mandatory for financial transactions, filing income tax returns, and opening bank accounts.
requiredDocuments: Form 49A (for Indian citizens) or Form 49AA (for foreign nationals)
Proof of Identity (Aadhaar, Voter ID, Passport, or Driving License)
Proof of Address (Aadhaar, Utility bill, Bank statement, or Passport)
Proof of Date of Birth (Birth certificate, Matriculation certificate, or Passport)
Passport-size photograph
steps: Visit the NSDL (onlineservices.nsdl.com) or UTIITSL (pan.utiitsl.com) website
Select 'New PAN' or 'Changes/Corrections in PAN' as applicable
Fill Form 49A online with personal and address details
Upload scanned documents and photograph
Pay the processing fee online
Submit and note the acknowledgment number
You can opt for e-PAN (delivered via email) or physical PAN card (delivered by post)
Track status using the acknowledgment number
whereToApply: NSDL e-Gov portal (onlineservices.nsdl.com) or UTIITSL portal (pan.utiitsl.com) or nearest PAN centre
estimatedTime: 15–20 days for physical card; 2–3 days for e-PAN
fees: ₹107 (for Indian address); ₹1,017 (for foreign address) — inclusive of GST

description: An Indian Passport is issued by the Ministry of External Affairs and is required for international travel. It also serves as a strong proof of identity and citizenship.
requiredDocuments: Aadhaar Card (mandatory for address verification under ePassport)
PAN Card or Voter ID or Driving License (additional ID proof)
Birth Certificate or Matriculation Certificate (proof of date of birth)
Address proof (Aadhaar, Utility bill, Bank statement, or Rent agreement)
Marriage Certificate (if applicable, for spouse name endorsement)
Old Passport (if reissue/renewal)
steps: Register on the Passport Seva Portal (passportindia.gov.in)
Fill the online application form and select the type (Fresh/Re-issue, Normal/Tatkal)
Pay the fee online
Book an appointment at the nearest Passport Seva Kendra (PSK) or Post Office Passport Seva Kendra (POPSK)
Visit the PSK on the appointment date with original documents
Biometrics (photo, fingerprints) will be captured at the PSK
Police verification will be initiated (may happen before or after passport dispatch)
Passport will be dispatched by Speed Post after police verification is cleared
whereToApply: Passport Seva Portal (passportindia.gov.in), nearest PSK, or POPSK
estimatedTime: Normal: 15–30 days; Tatkal: 1–7 days (subject to police verification)

description: A Driving License is issued by the Regional Transport Office (RTO) and is legally required to drive motor vehicles in India. It also serves as a valid proof of identity and address.
requiredDocuments: Application Form (Form 4 for Learner's License, Form 9 for permanent DL)
Aadhaar Card
Proof of Address (Aadhaar, Utility bill, or Passport)
Proof of Age (Birth certificate, SSLC certificate, or Passport)
Passport-size photographs
Medical certificate (Form 1A — from a registered medical practitioner)
Learner's License (must be held for at least 30 days before applying for permanent DL)
steps: Visit the Parivahan portal (parivahan.gov.in) or the Sarathi portal of your state
Apply for a Learner's License first — fill the form, upload documents, pay fee
Book a slot and appear for the Learner's License test at the RTO (theory test)
After receiving Learner's License, practice driving for at least 30 days
Apply for the permanent Driving License on the same portal
Book a slot for the driving test at the RTO
Pass the driving test
DL will be dispatched by post or can be downloaded as a digital DL via DigiLocker/mParivahan
whereToApply: Parivahan / Sarathi portal (parivahan.gov.in) or nearest RTO office
estimatedTime: Learner's License: 7–15 days. Permanent DL: 30 days after LL + 7–15 days processing
fees: Learner's License: ₹200–₹500. Permanent DL: ₹400–₹1,000 (varies by state and vehicle class). Renewal: ₹250–₹500

description: A Senior Citizen ID Card is issued by local municipal authorities or state social welfare departments to persons aged 60 years and above. It entitles holders to benefits like priority in queues, travel concessions, and access to welfare schemes.
requiredDocuments: Application form (from the Municipal office or Social Welfare department)
Aadhaar Card
Age proof (Birth certificate, Passport, Voter ID, or School certificate)
Proof of Address (Aadhaar, Utility bill, or Ration card)
Passport-size photographs (2–3)
Medical fitness certificate (in some states)
steps: Visit the local Municipal Corporation office or District Social Welfare office
Obtain and fill the Senior Citizen ID application form
Attach the required documents and photographs
Submit the form at the same office
After verification, the Senior Citizen ID card will be issued
Some states offer online application through their Social Welfare portal
whereToApply: District Social Welfare Office, Municipal Corporation, or state Social Welfare portal
fees: Free in most states; ₹10–₹50 in some states

description: A Marriage Certificate is a legal document proving the marriage between two persons. It is required for name change after marriage, spouse visa, joint bank accounts, and various legal and immigration purposes.
requiredDocuments: Application form
Aadhaar Card / ID proof of both spouses
Proof of Age of both spouses (Birth certificate, SSLC, or Passport)
Address proof of both spouses
Wedding invitation card or photographs of the wedding
Affidavit of marriage (on stamp paper, notarized)
Two witness ID proofs and passport-size photographs
Church/Temple/Mosque certificate (if applicable)
steps: Visit the local Sub-Registrar office or apply online through your state's registration portal
Fill the marriage registration form
Both spouses and witnesses must be present at the Sub-Registrar office on the appointment date
Submit all required documents and the marriage affidavit
Pay the registration fee
The Sub-Registrar verifies documents and records the marriage
Marriage Certificate is issued — collect from the office or download online
whereToApply: Local Sub-Registrar / Registrar of Marriages office or state online marriage registration portal
estimatedTime: 7–30 days (some states issue same day if all documents are in order)
fees: ₹100–₹500 (varies by state); additional stamp paper costs for affidavit

description: State pension schemes (Old Age Pension, Widow Pension, Disability Pension) provide monthly financial assistance to eligible citizens. Eligibility and amounts vary by state.
requiredDocuments: Application form
Aadhaar Card
Age proof (Birth certificate, Voter ID, or School certificate) — must be 60+ for old age pension
Income Certificate (to prove BPL/low-income status)
Bank account passbook (account must be Aadhaar-linked)
BPL Card / Ration Card (if applicable)
Disability certificate (for disability pension)
Death certificate of husband (for widow pension)
Passport-size photographs
steps: Visit the District Social Welfare Office or apply online on your state's pension portal
Fill the pension application form
Attach all required documents
Submit at the Social Welfare Office, Tehsildar office, or through CSC
A field verification may be conducted
After approval, pension amount is credited monthly to the linked bank account
Re-verification (life certificate / Jeevan Pramaan) is needed annually
whereToApply: District Social Welfare Office, Tehsildar office, Common Service Centre, or state pension portal
estimatedTime: 30–60 days for approval and first disbursement

serviceName: Ayushman Bharat - Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PMJAY)
description: Ayushman Bharat PMJAY provides health insurance coverage of up to ₹5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary hospitalization. It covers over 50 crore beneficiaries from economically weaker sections.
requiredDocuments: Aadhaar Card (mandatory for identity verification)
Ration Card / SECC (Socio-Economic Caste Census) data — eligibility is auto-determined based on SECC 2011 data
Any government-issued ID (if Aadhaar not available, some states accept Voter ID, etc.)
Mobile number
steps: Check eligibility at mera.pmjay.gov.in by entering your mobile number or Ration Card number
If eligible, visit the nearest Ayushman Bharat Kiosk, Common Service Centre (CSC), or empaneled hospital
Carry your Aadhaar Card and Ration Card for verification
Your identity will be verified and an Ayushman Card (e-card) will be generated
The card is family-based — all eligible family members are covered
At the time of hospitalization, show your Ayushman Card at any empaneled hospital
Treatment is cashless up to ₹5 lakh per year — no out-of-pocket payment needed
whereToApply: mera.pmjay.gov.in, nearest CSC, Ayushman Bharat Kiosk at empaneled hospitals, or state health department office
estimatedTime: Ayushman Card can be generated same day if documents are in order
fees: Completely free — no premium, no enrollment fee, no charges at empaneled hospitals
